title: Nizam Ecosystem Engineering Finding Schema
description: Validates a single engineering finding, per ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 7 (Audit Artifact) and docs/nips/NIP-0001-ecosystem-engineering-cycle.md: a repository- or ecosystem-scoped finding recorded in .agent/audits/<audit-id>/findings.json. Requires severity, confidence (the protocol's fixed Confirmed/Probable/Suspected vocabulary, Sec 5), evidence (externalised by path, per-item revision-pinned per ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 4's anchoring rule), impact, owner, and closure_criteria on every finding; requires a structured, non-empty closure_evidence whenever a finding's status is resolved (never for open findings), so a finding cannot be marked resolved without closure evidence.

evidence_item.description: A single, path-referenced piece of evidence, revision-pinned per ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 4 (an evidence item with no declared revision is not valid). Raw output is never pasted inline here (methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5); only the path and its revision anchor are recorded.

path.description: Path to the externalised evidence file, per ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 7 (evidence is externalised by path under .agent/evidence/<execution-id>/, never pasted inline).

revision.description: The repository (or artifact) revision this evidence item is anchored to, per ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 4's revision anchoring rule.

id.description: Unique identifier for this finding within its audit run.

severity.description: The finding's severity category. Deliberately NOT a fixed enum: ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 1 states consumers extend this protocol with their OWN repository- and ecosystem-specific finding categories and severities, and that this protocol does not redefine them (only its evidence hierarchy, maturity model, confidence vocabulary, and commercial-readiness exclusion are fixed, Sec 1). Required and non-empty so every finding names a severity, without this schema hardcoding a consumer-specific severity vocabulary the protocol explicitly reserves as an extension point.

confidence.description: The finding's confidence level. Exactly the protocol's fixed, closed vocabulary (ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 5): Confirmed (Tier 1 evidence), Probable (Tier 2), Suspected (Tier 3). Unlike severity, this vocabulary is NOT consumer-extensible -- the protocol defines it once, here.

maturity.description: OPTIONAL maturity-model claim the finding asserts, exactly one of the protocol's ten ascending levels (ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 4). Not every finding asserts a maturity claim (e.g. a finding solely about evidence or closure state may not), so this property is not required by any acceptance test in this feature; when present it MUST be one of the ten named levels, in the protocol's own spelling and order.

evidence.description: The evidence backing this finding (ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 3/7). At least one item; every item is path-referenced and revision-pinned (see $defs/evidence_item) -- a finding recorded with no evidence, or evidence missing a path or revision, is not valid.

impact.description: The finding's engineering impact -- what is affected, and how, if the underlying condition is not addressed.

owner.description: The identity (person, team, or role) accountable for the finding until it is closed.

status.description: The finding's current lifecycle state within this audit run. 'resolved' triggers the closure_evidence requirement below (see allOf); this is a within-finding lifecycle flag, distinct from ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md's own new/resolved/reopened/stale classification of findings ACROSS two compared executions (that protocol's vocabulary governs comparisons of finding SETS, not a single finding's own required fields, and is out of this feature's scope).

closure_criteria.description: The criteria that must be met for this finding to be considered closed (ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 7). Required on every finding, regardless of current status, so a finding always states what 'done' looks like -- distinct from closure_evidence, which records that those criteria were actually met.

closure_evidence.description: Evidence demonstrating the closure_criteria were met. Required, and non-empty, ONLY when status is 'resolved' (see allOf below); a finding marked resolved with no closure_evidence, or with a closure_evidence array present but containing only structurally-incomplete items (missing path or revision), is not valid.
